name: Ken's Family Bedroom
location: Ginza District, Tokyo, Japan - Urban Apartment Bedroom
layout: Compact rectangular room with a double bed positioned against the far wall, flanked by a wooden nightstand on the left and a built-in wardrobe on the right; sliding glass door to a small balcony at the back providing depth; low-profile dresser near the entrance forming the foreground; neutral partitioning creates clear foreground (entry area with dresser), midground (bed and nightstands), and background (balcony view with city skyline silhouette).
materials: Smooth white plaster walls with subtle texture; polished hardwood flooring in dark oak tones; minimalist furniture in matte walnut veneer and white lacquer; sheer linen curtains on balcony door; frosted glass accents on wardrobe doors; soft cotton bedding in neutral grays.
lighting: Soft ambient light from a recessed ceiling fixture diffusing evenly across the room; subtle blue-tinted glow from city lights filtering through balcony curtains at night; single bedside lamp with warm LED bulb casting gentle shadows on the near wall; no harsh overhead spots, maintaining stable and realistic light distribution.
atmosphere: Quiet and introspective with a sense of urban isolation; low-frequency hum of distant city traffic implied through subtle visual cues like faint light pollution; cool temperature evoked by minimalist decor and indirect lighting; layered depth enhances suspenseful tension without overt drama.
establishing_prompt: Empty modern Japanese apartment bedroom in Ginza, Tokyo, at night: compact space with double bed against far wall, wooden nightstand and wardrobe on sides, dresser near entrance, sliding balcony door with sheer curtains overlooking dim city skyline; smooth white walls, dark oak floors, matte furniture; soft diffused ceiling light and blue city glow, realistic cinematic style, wide-angle empty room reference image.
